commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f0a65a1dd4fc3402264dbed21ce80882d3271c5a)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a bundled Ask plugin with a Discord `/ask` command, a new `plugin-sdk/discord-interactions` subpath, Discord native-command component reply handling, tests, docs, and workspace metadata.

Reproducibility: yes. for the main review finding: source inspection shows unscoped plugin commands support every channel and `nativeNames.default` applies cross-provider. Live `/ask` behavior itself has not been reproduced because the PR only provides build/dist proof.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer product/API judgment and contributor real-behavior proof; this is not a safe automation-only repair lane.

target.title: feat: add minimal Discord /ask command
target.body:
## Summary
- add bundled ask plugin with a Discord native /ask command
- choose button/select/modal from the prompt and store log-only ask sessions/feedback
- expose a focused discord-interactions plugin-sdk subpath and register native command reply components so Discord callbacks reach plugin interactive handlers

## Safety
- /ask is answer-capture only: nextActionPolicy=log_only, requiresSecondGo=true, actionScope=answer_capture_only
- default allowedUsers is the invoking sender, components are non-reusable, sessions expire after 30 minutes
- no Poll routing, Select+Modal composite, approval execution, external send, config mutation, or long-running task execution in this PR

## Verification
- pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/ask/index.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/native-command-reply.test.ts
- PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH pnpm tsgo:extensions
- node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-subpath-exports.mjs
- node scripts/sync-plugin-sdk-exports.mjs --check
- node scripts/check-extension-package-tsc-boundary.mjs --mode=compile
- PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH pnpm build:strict-smoke

## Notes
- Local shell did not have a global pnpm binary, so a temporary shim to corepack pnpm was used for package scripts that shell out to pnpm.
- build:strict-smoke emitted existing zod locale CommonJS dts warnings from rolldown-plugin-dts, but completed successfully.

## Real behavior proof

After the fix, I built the actual OpenClaw runtime locally from this branch and verified the generated dist artifacts that the gateway/package runtime would load.

```text
$ PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH pnpm build:strict-smoke
CLI bootstrap import guard passed.
runtime-postbuild: bundled plugin metadata completed
runtime-postbuild: bundled plugin runtime overlay completed
OK: All 4 required plugin-sdk exports verified.

$ node - <<'NODE'
OK dist/extensions/ask/index.js
OK dist/extensions/ask/openclaw.plugin.json
OK dist/plugin-sdk/discord-interactions.js
OK dist/plugin-sdk/discord-interactions.d.ts
manifest id=ask name=Ask
export discord-interactions=true
NODE

$ node dist/index.js --version
OpenClaw 2026.5.19 (7ea06fb)
```

This is not a mocked unit test: it verifies the real built dist output includes the bundled ask plugin and the new plugin-sdk export used by the runtime. Live Discord smoke still belongs after merge/install because the currently running global package is the published runtime and has not been replaced by this PR build.
target.author: ximanuki
